context_note: Normal cycle following May 24 scan. Carry-forward items from May 24: Turkey CHP physical HQ seizure (post-apply status question), Hungary EU funds agreement signing window open this week (May 29 target), Poland KRS reconstitution (sub-threshold accumulator direction now contested), Italy first-baseline Dismantler assessment overdue, Colombia OHCHR sub-threshold accumulator (-3.5). New signals discovered in May 25 scan: (1) Slovakia — European Parliament passed 418-207 resolution on May 20 urging EU conditionality mechanism to freeze €15B in Slovak funds — MAJOR ESCALATION beyond what was documented in May 22 assessment; (2) Turkey — Time magazine 'deepening democratic crisis' explainer, massive street protests documented, pro-Kurdish DEM Party condemnation — protest mobilization ongoing; (3) Palestine/Gaza — HRW May 19 report: Israel curbs aid, kills civilians during ceasefire — 856 Palestinians killed, only 50% of aid trucks allowed to unload; (4) Ukraine — Russia and Ukraine declaring competing ceasefires (May 4), Trump 3-day ceasefire (May 9-11), Putin suggests war 'coming to an end' (May 10), Geneva talks stalled — complex ceasefire/negotiation arc affecting Ukraine score; (5) Kenya — Meta terminated Sama content moderation contract (retaliation allegation by workers), Global Trade Union Alliance of Content Moderators launched in Nairobi — EMP-dimension signals for Kenya first-baseline; (6) Indonesia — ban on Grok conditionally lifted February 2026, meaning the positive governance action is now partially reversed. DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic-Pentagon still pending (cycle 6 hold).

turkey_post_apply_escalation.status: ESCALATION CONFIRMED AND DEEPENING — Protests now massive; Time magazine 'democratic crisis' explainer; pro-Kurdish DEM Party condemns; Özel led march to Parliament
turkey_post_apply_escalation.finding: On May 25, Time published a major explainer titled 'What to Know About Türkiye's Deepening Democratic Crisis', confirming the May 24 police action as internationally recognized as a democratic crisis event. Turkey's pro-Kurdish DEM Party has formally condemned the ousting of CHP leadership and the police operation, calling it 'a disgrace to democracy'. CHP leader Özel emerged from the headquarters on May 24 and led a march toward Parliament (6 km). Massive street protests are documented across Turkey. The scope of international coverage has escalated significantly since the May 24 scan. This confirms: the physical seizure of CHP headquarters is a widely recognized discrete democratic crisis event, not merely a footnote to the May 21 court ruling. Assessor must determine whether the scale of protest mobilization and international characterization as 'deepening democratic crisis' constitutes additional scored evidence beyond the May 23 apply (-4.9, now at 17.6 working baseline).

hungary_eu_funds_agreement.status: WINDOW ACTIVE — Signing target now May 29 (Thursday). Magyar confirmed returning to Brussels to finalize agreement.
hungary_eu_funds_agreement.finding: Magyar stated on May 23 that negotiations are 'progressing well' and the signing is expected Thursday (May 29) in Brussels. This is the most time-sensitive forward trigger in the current cycle — if signed May 29, the Hungary upgrade thesis transitions from 'reforms-announced' to 'reforms-formally-committed-with-EC-enforcement-mechanism.' Sulyok refuses to resign (confirmed May 18); Magyar's May 31 constitutional amendment threat remains active. Both axes must be tracked through end of May.

slovakia_ep_conditionality_vote.status: NEW MAJOR EVENT — European Parliament 418-207 resolution May 20 urging EU conditionality mechanism against Slovakia — SIGNIFICANT ESCALATION
slovakia_ep_conditionality_vote.finding: The European Parliament passed a resolution on May 20, 2026, with 418 votes in favor and 207 against, formally urging the European Commission to trigger the conditionality mechanism and potentially freeze Slovakia's €15B in EU funds. This is a formal parliamentary action — stronger than a political statement but not yet a Commission decision. The resolution cites: dissolution of the special prosecution office, cuts to corruption penalties, restrictions on independent media and civil society, and misuse of Agricultural Paying Agency funds. The Renew Europe Group (May 20) called for investigation into EU funds misuse including 'guesthouse projects' where EU funds financed luxury private estates. This event occurred after Slovakia's May 22 assessment in this pipeline and must be evaluated for a new apply cycle. Slovakia was assessed at 39.1 Developing band on May 22. The EP resolution 418-207 is a Tier-1 EU institutional action directly referencing rule-of-law concerns about Fico's government.

anthropic_dc_circuit.status: PENDING — Cycle 6 hold. No ruling issued as of May 25. Oral arguments were May 19. Ruling expected within weeks.
anthropic_dc_circuit.finding: No DC Circuit ruling has issued. Strong panel skepticism of DoD position remains on record (Judge Henderson: 'spectacular overreach'). Band-crossing-proposed hold continues. Anthropic working baseline: 60.0. Cycle 6 hold.

poland_krs.status: HOLD — No new Poland-specific evidence May 25. KRS reconstitution event stands as documented from May 18. Sub-threshold direction remains contested.
poland_krs.finding: No new KRS-related events in the May 25 window. The sub-threshold accumulator from 40.2 remains direction-contested: -1.8 downward accumulator vs. positive KRS reconstitution counter-signal. Nawrocki presidency expected to be a sustained obstacle. Poland at 0.2 above Developing/Functional boundary. No evidence of resolution this cycle. Carry-forward to next assessor cycle.

top_entities.name: Turkey
top_entities.note: RS not yet updated with May 23 apply (-4.9). Working baseline 17.6. Post-apply escalation arc now confirmed by international media as 'deepening democratic crisis.' Protest mobilization continues.
top_entities.news_summary: May 25 confirms the Turkey arc has entered a new register. Time magazine published a 'Deepening Democratic Crisis' explainer on May 25. Turkey's pro-Kurdish DEM Party formally condemned the ousting as 'a disgrace to democracy.' CHP leader Özel led a march toward Parliament. Massive street protests documented across Turkey. The Organiser reports protests against 'dictatorship of Erdogan.' The framing has shifted from a discrete legal event (May 21 court ruling) to an internationally recognized multi-day governance crisis involving: judicial removal of leadership, police occupation of opposition headquarters, and mass public protest. Context: Istanbul Mayor Imamoglu has been imprisoned since March 2025 and is on trial for corruption. The arrest, the court ruling, and the headquarters seizure form a three-part arc of opposition suppression. Turkey's working baseline is 17.6 (Critical band, post-May 23 apply of -4.9).
top_entities.assessor_note: May 23 apply was -4.9 (30.8→17.6). Working baseline 17.6. The critical assessment question remains: does the May 24 physical headquarters seizure constitute a second discrete scored event? The May 25 evidence adds: (1) Mass protest mobilization — Turkey-wide protests against the actions, confirming broad societal reaction; (2) International framing as 'deepening democratic crisis' (Time, multiple outlets); (3) DEM Party condemnation — cross-partisan domestic institutional response. New threshold question: if protest suppression events occur (arrests of protesters, use of violence against marchers), these would constitute additional HAR-dimension events beyond the governance arc. Assessor should watch for protest suppression in the next 48-72 hours. Current recommendation: document the arc continuation, determine whether protest mobilization + international 'democratic crisis' framing crosses a second apply threshold. Turkey floor: no explicit benchmark floor below 0 in the scale, but 17.6 Critical band already represents severe status. Any additional apply from 17.6 must be proportionally calibrated.

top_entities.name: Slovakia
top_entities.note: Slovakia was last assessed May 22 (cycle applied -5.5 from Liberties Dismantler classification). New event: May 20 EP resolution 418-207 urging conditionality mechanism — this event post-dates the May 22 assessment.
top_entities.news_summary: NEW MAJOR EVENT POST-ASSESSMENT: On May 20, 2026, the European Parliament voted 418-207 to formally urge the European Commission to trigger the EU Rule of Law Conditionality Mechanism against Slovakia — a step that could potentially freeze Slovakia's €15 billion in EU funds. The vote was driven by: dissolution of the special prosecution office; cuts to corruption penalties; abolition of the Whistleblower Protection Office attempt; restrictions on independent media and civil society; alleged misuse of EU agricultural funds and 'guesthouse projects' (EU funds financing luxury private estates with minimal public benefit). Renew Europe Group and leading MEPs cited a 'manual for autocrats' pattern. This is the first time the conditionality mechanism — applied against Hungary under Orbán — has been actively urged for Slovakia. The Commission now faces political pressure to open a formal investigation. Note: the EP resolution is urging, not triggering — the Commission decides. But 418-207 is a very strong political signal that post-dates the May 22 assessment and must be evaluated for a second apply.
top_entities.assessor_note: Slovakia was assessed at 39.1 on May 22 (first formal Dismantler apply, -5.5 from 44.6). The EP 418-207 conditionality vote on May 20 is a new event — stronger than a political statement, weaker than a Commission decision. For scoring purposes: peer precedent is Hungary, where the conditionality mechanism was used and eventually resulted in EU fund freezes. Slovakia at 39.1 Developing band sits 0.9 below Functional floor (40.0). A second apply from this EP action could push Slovakia further into Developing band or toward the Developing/Critical boundary at 20.0 — but 39.1 is far from that boundary, so the methodological question is whether the EP urging (without Commission action yet) crosses the 5pt formal-apply threshold. Assessor should assess: (1) EP 418-207 resolution on conditionality — is this Tier-1 EU institutional action that triggers a scored event, or is it accumulation pending Commission decision? (2) Note that Commission action would take weeks/months; the EP resolution itself may qualify as a new institutional signal above the accumulation threshold. Sub-threshold accumulation candidate: +5 for EU institutional escalation. Hold at 39.1 pending Commission response unless assessor determines EP vote is independently apply-threshold.

top_entities.name: Hungary
top_entities.news_summary: Signing window now explicit: May 29 (Thursday). Magyar confirmed to von der Leyen he would return to Brussels 'the week of May 25' — Magyar's May 23 statement upgraded this to 'next Thursday'. The agreement covers anti-corruption, EPPO membership, judicial independence, and press freedom — the four reform pillars that caused €10.4B in RRF funds to be frozen under Orbán. If signed May 29: upgrade thesis transitions from 'reforms-announced' to 'reforms-formally-committed-with-EC-enforcement-mechanism.' Secondary signal: Sulyok refuses to resign (May 18); Magyar threatens constitutional amendment by May 31. The constitutional confrontation creates governance uncertainty even as the EU funds agreement represents a major positive. No reversal of reform commitment documented in this scan window.
top_entities.assessor_note: CRITICAL FORWARD TRIGGER: May 29 signing window. Assessor must monitor for confirmation before proposing any score change. If signed May 29 or before next cycle: formal apply upward warranted — 'reforms-committed-with-EC-mechanism' is a materially stronger governance signal than prior 'reforms-announced' baseline. If unsigned or delayed: extend hold and document. Sulyok May 31 deadline is simultaneously active: if Magyar moves on constitutional amendment, assess whether that is a positive democratic action (supermajority removing an Orbán-era Fidesz holdover) or a governance-dimension concern (executive override of institutional constitutional design). Working baseline: 47.7.

top_entities.name: Palestine
top_entities.note: Palestine is a carry-forward editorial hold requiring founder decision. The May 25 scan finds new HRW institutional evidence post-dating prior scan cycles. 72,619+ Palestinians killed as of May 6, 2026. Aid being blocked during ceasefire.
top_entities.news_summary: NEW HRW INSTITUTIONAL EVIDENCE (May 19, 2026): Human Rights Watch published 'Gaza: Israel Curbs Aid, Kills Civilians During Ceasefire' — documenting that (1) at least 856 Palestinians and 2,463 wounded since the October 2025 ceasefire; (2) In the first 11 days of May 2026, only 50% of aid trucks from Egypt were allowed to unload at Israeli-controlled crossings; (3) On February 28, 2026, when Israeli-US military operations against Iran began, Israel closed all Gaza crossings — weekly truck average fell from 4,200 to 590; (4) OHCHR High Commissioner Türk stated in April 2026: Palestinians across Gaza unsafe six months after ceasefire announcement. OCHA May 1 report: only 10% of 2026 Flash Appeal ($4 billion) funded; nearly all of 2.1 million population displaced. Additionally: ICJ has ruled Israel's aid ban inconsistent with international law; ICC arrest warrants issued for Netanyahu and Gallant. Total documented deaths: 72,619+ Palestinians from October 7, 2023 through May 6, 2026.
top_entities.assessor_note: Palestine remains a cycle 6+ editorial hold requiring founder decision. The May 25 scan adds fresh institutional evidence: HRW May 19 report specifically documenting aid curbs during ceasefire, civilian killings during ceasefire, and OHCHR April 2026 statement confirming ongoing unsafe conditions. This is not a first-scan; it is accumulating evidence for a pending founder decision. The benchmark must determine: (1) Is the Palestinian Authority (PA) the scored entity, or is the entity Gaza-as-administered-territory? (2) What is the anchor composite — Palestine cannot be scored purely on actions of an occupying power, but Israeli actions directly determine Palestinian welfare outcomes at a scale that any scoring system must address. New evidence this cycle: 856 killed during ceasefire (ceasefire in effect but civilians still dying), aid deliberately blocked, ICJ ruling that the ban is illegal, ICC warrants for Netanyahu/Gallant, OCHA 10% funding gap. Document for founder decision queue.

top_entities.name: Italy
top_entities.news_summary: Italy first-baseline Dismantler assessment remains overdue. No new Italy-specific events in May 25 scan window beyond what was documented May 24. Key evidence arc remains: Liberties 2026 Dismantler classification (March 30); anti-SLAPP transposition deadline missed May 7 (Italy formally the EU country with highest number of SLAPP cases per EU Parliament 2024 study); CIVICUS downgrade (narrowed→obstructed); Nordio judicial reform rejected by referendum 53.5% No, 46.5% Yes (positive democratic offset); Meloni personal SLAPP against Saviano documented. Italy at 50.0 Functional. First-baseline Dismantler assessment expected to apply downward delta, likely -3 to -5 range crediting referendum positive offset. No band-crossing event unless delta exceeds 10 points.
top_entities.assessor_note: Carry-forward from May 24. No new events May 25. First-baseline Dismantler assessment is overdue — Italy is the last unassessed EU Dismantler from the Liberties 2026 Report. The article19.org evidence (Italy Go Beyond Anti-SLAPP directive) and the TEPSA analysis reinforce the media freedom erosion dimension. Assessor should proceed with first-baseline assessment using accumulated evidence. Expected range: -3 to -5 from 50.0 (47-45 range). Methodology precedent from May 25 scan: Italy is the EU country with the highest number of SLAPP cases in the EU (EU Parliament 2024 study) — this strengthens the AWR-dimension negative weight.

top_entities.name: Poland
top_entities.news_summary: No new Poland events in May 25 window. KRS reconstitution event (May 18) stands as documented. The sub-threshold accumulator direction (-1.8 downward from 40.2) is now contested by the positive KRS reconstitution counter-signal. Nawrocki's victory in the presidential election creates a 5-year structural obstacle to rule-of-law reforms. The May 25 search found Nawrocki vetoed the Tusk judicial reform bill (February 19, 2026), confirming the veto/alternative-path dynamic. The CT injunction against the KRS vote was defied — the Tusk government deliberately chose the alternative path. Poland at 40.2, sitting 0.2 above Developing/Functional boundary. No resolution this cycle.
top_entities.assessor_note: Carry-forward from May 24. No new events. The KRS reconstitution positive counter-signal has not yet been formally assessed. Assessor must weigh: (1) Does the KRS reconstitution (judiciary-nominated members) cross the apply threshold as a positive event, potentially offsetting the prior -1.8 downward accumulator? (2) Poland's Nawrocki presidency (5 years) creates a structural-negative signal for future reform trajectories — this alone may not be apply-threshold but is a multi-year governance downward context signal. Boundary proximity (0.2 above Developing/Functional) means the assessor must be precise about which direction the KRS event pushes. Hold at 40.2 pending full assessment of competing KRS signals.

top_entities.name: Amazon
top_entities.note: RS shows 17.8 (pre-May 23 apply). Working baseline 12.8 per May 23 formal apply (-5.0). NLRB enforcement arc is multi-facility and ongoing.
top_entities.news_summary: No new Amazon-specific events beyond what was documented in May 24. The NLRB enforcement arc remains: Teamsters first-ever bargaining order (April 2026); NLRB Region 29 federal court cease-and-desist on protected-activity firings; Bellingham APWU formal charge; May 2026 Eleventh Circuit hearing on Amazon's challenge to NLRB meeting rule. The Eleventh Circuit (May 2026) is now hearing arguments over NLRB's ban on company anti-union meetings — Amazon is actively litigating to limit NLRB process authority even as it faces multiple enforcement actions. The combined enforcement arc confirms systemic multi-facility EMP-dimension violations. Amazon working baseline: 12.8 (Critical).
top_entities.assessor_note: May 24 carry-forward. The Eleventh Circuit argument (Amazon challenging NLRB meeting rule) is a new accumulation signal: Amazon is not only violating labor law, it is actively litigating to curtail NLRB enforcement authority. This is a second-order governance signal — attempting to weaken the regulatory body that is enforcing against it. Assessor should determine: do the multi-facility NLRB enforcement actions (Teamsters bargaining order + federal court cease-and-desist + Bellingham charge) constitute a second apply-threshold event on top of the May 23 apply? Working baseline 12.8. Second apply would push deeper into Critical band.

top_entities.name: Ukraine
top_entities.note: Ukraine at 50.0 (Functional). May 25 scan finds major new ceasefire/peace arc that was not covered in prior scan cycles. Last assessed May 17. Sub-threshold change proposal pending from May 7.
top_entities.news_summary: MAJOR NEW ARC — Russia-Ukraine Ceasefire and Peace Negotiations: (1) Russia and Ukraine declared competing 3-day ceasefires (May 4, 2026); (2) Trump announced a 3-day ceasefire (May 9-11) including prisoner exchange of 1,000 per side; (3) Putin suggested on May 10 that Russia's war on Ukraine is 'coming to an end'; (4) Geneva peace talks (Feb 17-18, 2026) — military portions nearing completion, but political negotiations 'stuck' on Donbas territorial control. Ukraine working toward peace agreement while Russian demands for full Donbas control remain unacceptable to Zelenskyy without referendum. The ceasefire arc represents a significant positive signal for Ukraine's humanitarian dimension — civilians getting relief periods, prisoner exchanges occurring. However, war continues; Russian military operations ongoing; Geneva talks stalled on territorial issues. Net assessment: humanitarian improvements (ceasefire periods) vs. ongoing military/political stalemate.
top_entities.assessor_note: Ukraine at 50.0 Functional. The ceasefire arc is a new positive humanitarian signal — 3-day ceasefire implemented May 9-11 with prisoner exchanges, reducing immediate civilian harm. However, peace talks remain stalled on territorial questions. Ukraine is not the aggressor in this conflict; the ceasefire events reflect Ukraine's posture as a party willing to reduce harm when conditions allow. Assessor should determine: (1) Does the ceasefire/prisoner exchange arc constitute a positive apply-threshold signal for Ukraine's composite? (2) Is the ongoing military stalemate and Geneva talks impasse a neutral hold or a negative signal for Ukraine's welfare-delivery capacity? Sub-threshold change proposal from May 7 is still pending — assessor should address both the May 7 pending item and the new ceasefire arc in the next formal assessment.

top_entities.name: Anthropic
top_entities.news_summary: No DC Circuit ruling issued as of May 25. Band-crossing-proposed hold continues (cycle 6). No new developments. The court record continues to document: Judge Henderson described DoD action as 'spectacular overreach.' Ruling expected within weeks. Anthropic working baseline: 60.0 (exactly at Functional/Established boundary). The contrast with xAI/Grok's CSAM arc remains a qualitative context anchor — Anthropic was blacklisted for refusing to enable lethal autonomous weapons; xAI faces DSA investigation for generating CSAM. The two arcs represent opposite poles of AI lab safety governance.
top_entities.assessor_note: Cycle 6 band-crossing-proposed hold. No new evidence. Carry forward until DC Circuit ruling. If ruling issues against DoD: band-crossing (Functional→Established at 60.0) confirmed. If ruling is delayed beyond June: reassess whether hold should continue or be upgraded to a provisional determination. No change recommended this cycle.

top_entities.name: Microsoft
top_entities.news_summary: No new Microsoft-specific events in May 25 window. Rule of 70 voluntary buyout program (8,750 eligible employees, 7% of US workforce) remains active — 30-day acceptance window running through early June 2026. Microsoft is the first AI lab evaluated at CAISI (Commerce AI Center) alongside Google DeepMind and xAI — model security evaluations in classified environments. No Tech for Apartheid worker demands against Microsoft remain on record. Sub-threshold accumulator -1.9 from published 65.3 remains. Microsoft is also described as 'replaceable senior earners' in Rule of 70 coverage — raising age-discrimination risk questions though program is framed as voluntary.
top_entities.assessor_note: Sub-threshold accumulator -1.9 from 65.3. The Rule of 70 buyout program is EMP-dimension — voluntary framing reduces severity, but 8,750 eligible employees targeting middle management / senior earners disproportionately is a structural workforce signal. The CAISI AI model evaluation (classified environment) is a transparency-dimension positive signal — Microsoft cooperating with government safety evaluations. Net balance: buyout adds to downward accumulation; CAISI cooperation may partially offset. Does accumulated total (Israel-Azure + buyout) cross 5pt apply threshold from 65.3? Assessor must determine. Microsoft at 65.3 has substantial buffer from band boundaries.

top_entities.name: Colombia
top_entities.note: RS stale at 33.9. Working baseline 44.5 per May 6 assessment. Sub-threshold -3.5 accumulator. OHCHR Expert Mechanism country visit confirmed for May 4-13, 2026.
top_entities.news_summary: OHCHR Expert Mechanism (EMLER) visit to Colombia confirmed for May 4-13, 2026, visiting Bogotá, Cali, Cartagena, and Quibdó. Delegation met Vice President Francia Márquez and other officials. This is the Tier-1 evidence anchor — the preliminary findings of systematic racism against people of African descent issued May 14 were based on this physical country visit. OHCHR also documents: 410 human rights defenders killed between 2022-2025 (average ~100/year); three of eight OHCHR Colombia offices now closed, leaving many defenders — especially women — without protection. The closure of OHCHR offices is a new-to-this-scan structural capacity concern.
top_entities.assessor_note: Sub-threshold accumulator -3.5 from 44.5. New detail: three OHCHR Colombia offices closed — this reduces institutional oversight capacity (a governance-minus signal). The EMLER country visit (May 4-13) confirms the preliminary findings were based on direct on-the-ground evidence. The 410 human rights defenders killed 2022-2025 (100/year average) is a HAR-dimension data point for the accumulator. Assessor must determine: does the combined OHCHR evidence (systemic racism preliminary finding + human rights defenders violence + OHCHR office closures) cross the 5pt apply threshold from 44.5? If so, a formal apply from 44.5 would likely land below 40.0 (Developing boundary), requiring band-crossing protocol.

top_entities.name: Pakistan
top_entities.note: RS drift alert: RS 15.3 vs. working baseline 20.3. Band-crossing-proposed downward (Developing→Critical) pending founder decision.
top_entities.news_summary: New May 25 scan evidence: RSF (Reporters Without Borders) documented transnational repression against Pakistani journalists — four journalists in exile sentenced to life imprisonment in absentia for 'inciting violence' during 2023 Imran Khan arrest protests. OHCHR April 2026: called for release of human rights defender Idris Khattak with life-threatening health risk. HRW World Report 2026: documented 689 PECA cases (January-August period), systematic suppression of journalists and civil society. Tom Lantos Human Rights Commission held hearing titled 'Pakistan: Ongoing Political Repression.' The transnational persecution (life sentences in absentia for exiled journalists) adds a new repression dimension not fully documented in prior cycles.
top_entities.assessor_note: Band-crossing-proposed downward (Developing→Critical) pending founder decision from May 23 cycle. New evidence strengthens the downward case: transnational persecution (life sentences in absentia for exiled journalists) is a SYS-dimension event — attempting to suppress dissent even outside borders. OHCHR April 2026 individual human rights defender case adds ACC-dimension (government ignoring urgent OHCHR release demand). RS drift alert unresolved. No new evidence changes the recommendation direction. Founder decision required before formal apply.

top_entities.name: xAI/Grok
top_entities.note: RS and canonical both show 0. The EU DSA investigation is ongoing. May 25 scan confirms multi-jurisdiction regulatory arc is documented. xAI is also one of three AI labs being evaluated at CAISI Commerce AI Center — alongside Microsoft and Google DeepMind.
top_entities.news_summary: EU Commission DSA investigation (January 26, 2026) ongoing — EC ordered X to retain all Grok internal documents through end of 2026. 23,000 CSAM images generated in 11 days documented. Up to 6% annual global revenue fine if found in breach. CAISI (Commerce AI Center) is now also evaluating xAI models in classified environments — alongside Microsoft and Google DeepMind. This is a notable regulatory transparency signal: the US government is requiring security evaluations of xAI systems. No new escalation in the 24-hour window beyond carry-forward arc. RS composite 0 — assessor must reconcile whether this represents accurate minimum scoring or a data artifact.
top_entities.assessor_note: RS composite 0 — if accurate, xAI/Grok is at the floor of the benchmark scale. Assessor must confirm: (1) Is 0.0 a formally assessed composite or a data-entry artifact? (2) The CAISI evaluation of xAI models (classified environment, security evaluations) is a dual-signal: on one hand, government cooperation with security review is a transparency-positive; on the other hand, xAI being placed in the same CAISI evaluation cohort as Microsoft and Google DeepMind suggests US government views xAI as requiring security scrutiny commensurate with larger labs. Net: CAISI evaluation is neutral-to-slightly-positive for xAI but does not offset the CSAM arc. Sub-threshold -1.4 accumulator from prior cycles — reconcile with 0.0 RS.

top_entities.name: Kenya
top_entities.note: Upgraded from rotation to assess priority due to new Meta/Sama contract termination evidence and Global Content Moderators Alliance launch. Never formally assessed.
top_entities.news_summary: NEW MAY 2026 EVIDENCE ELEVATES KENYA: (1) Meta terminated its Sama contract in Kenya (April-May 2026) — ending the long-standing content moderation and AI training outsourcing arrangement; worker rights group alleges termination was retaliatory (for employees speaking out); Meta claims Sama 'failed to meet standards'; (2) The Global Trade Union Alliance of Content Moderators was launched in Nairobi, Kenya, representing workers from 9 countries; (3) Kenya's Artificial Intelligence Bill, 2026 introduced — the country's first AI governance framework, but bill excludes workers/trade unions from formal AI governance structures; (4) Jacobin (May 2026) cited Kenya in 'Big Tech's Cross-Border Abuses Demand International Response'; (5) Africa Tech Workers Rising Kenya project documented at Paris Peace Forum. Kenya at 41.8 Functional, 1.8pt above Functional floor. First-baseline assessment now warranted with this level of EMP-dimension evidence.
top_entities.assessor_note: Kenya elevated from rotation to assess priority due to new evidence. First-baseline assessment due. Evidence for assessment: (1) Meta/Sama contract termination — EMP-dimension for Kenya as a country where AI labor exploitation occurs with limited regulatory recourse; (2) AI Bill 2026 — positive governance signal (first AI framework) but incomplete (excludes worker voice); (3) Global Content Moderators Alliance launching in Nairobi — worker self-organization as a partial positive offset; (4) Broader context: Kenya documented as a major site of AI 'ghost work' with sub-$2/hour wages and psychological trauma. Assessor: first-baseline at 41.8 Functional — assess whether the evidence warrants an upward (AI governance positive) or downward (AI labor exploitation negative) delta, or a complex-signal hold.

top_entities.name: Canada
top_entities.note: 28 days since last assessment (April 27). Near Exemplary boundary (80.0). EMRIP technical advice confirmed May 7.
top_entities.news_summary: EMRIP Technical Advice confirmed May 7, 2026: UN Expert Mechanism on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples formally advised Canada to eliminate the second-generation cut-off in the Indian Act (assimilatory discrimination, UNDRIP violation). This is the second 2026 UN call — UN Human Rights Committee (March 2026) also recommended eliminating the discriminatory effects of the Indian Act. UBCIC (Union of BC Indian Chiefs) issued statement echoing UN calls and demanding Canada act on Bill S-2 amendments. CBC News covered EMRIP advice with First Nations advocate responses. Canada at 79.6, sitting 0.4 below the Exemplary boundary (80.0). Two concurrent UN institutional bodies calling for Indian Act reform creates a boundary-relevant accumulation signal: could push Canada from 79.6 upward if reforms are adopted, or contribute to a downward accumulator if Canada fails to act on dual UN recommendations.
top_entities.assessor_note: 28 days stale. Near Exemplary boundary (79.6, 0.4 below 80.0). Two UN bodies in 2026 calling for Indian Act reform creates a clear accumulation pattern. Canadian Human Rights Tribunal $8.5B Ontario First Nations reform agreement (March 2026) is a positive reparative signal. Assessor must weigh: dual UN recommendations (EMRIP May 7 + HRCO March) against the positive CHRT agreement. Net direction: likely accumulates toward boundary engagement. If Bill S-2 amendment passes or a formal government commitment is made: could push to 80.0 (Exemplary boundary). If government fails to act: downward pressure. Canada assessment is boundary-sensitive.

top_entities.name: Mexico
top_entities.news_summary: May 16, 2026 Boston Globe/Washington Times reporting: cartel drone warfare in Guerrero (Los Ardillos, May mid-May) — drones fired explosives at civilians, killed livestock, burned homes; 1,200 military/police deployed. Government displacement figures suppressed: NGOs document 44,695+ displaced 2024-2025; no comprehensive state registry exists (unlike Colombia). Sheinbaum explicitly rejected military offensive against cartels, citing human rights concerns. International Business Times UK: Mexico government 'rejects all-out cartel war' while prioritizing human rights over total victory framing. The May 2026 reporting confirms the invisible displacement crisis documented in May 23 carry-forward. No government registry = institutional capacity gap. First-baseline assessment remains due.
top_entities.assessor_note: Never formally assessed. First-baseline due. Evidence: cartel drone warfare, invisible displacement crisis, government suppression of figures, Sheinbaum judicial reform (popular election of judges), UN criticism. Mexico at 30.8 Developing. First-baseline assessment expected to explore range 25-30. The Sheinbaum anti-military stance on cartel response is a humanitarian complexity: refusing military offensive may be a human rights positive (avoiding civilian harm from military operations) while simultaneously allowing cartel violence to continue without effective deterrence. Assessor must weigh both dimensions of the cartel response policy.

rotation_backfill.name: Brazil
rotation_backfill.rotation_rationale: Never assessed. COP30 was held in Belém, November 2025 — outcomes include Indigenous rights recognition in three COP documents (land rights, traditional knowledge), $4B+ climate finance package, but no firm fossil fuel transition commitment. Latin America described as 'most dangerous region for environmental defenders.' First-baseline assessment anchored by COP30 legacy outcomes.

rotation_backfill.name: Vanuatu
rotation_backfill.rotation_rationale: Post-apply boundary watch (1.4pt above Functional floor at 40.0). May 31 Pacific cluster second resolution monitoring deadline. No new evidence this cycle. Document post-apply status confirmation and monitor.

rotation_backfill.name: United States
rotation_backfill.rotation_rationale: RS stale at 25.0 vs. working baseline 49.2 (post May 21 downgrade). OBBBA signed July 4, 2025 — implementation milestones now being tracked for 2026. Medicaid work requirement re-verification starts December 2026. CBO projection: 11.8M lose health insurance. Immigration: 605,000+ deported. Active accumulation but no single new apply-threshold event in May 25 window. Rotation documentation of OBBBA implementation arc.

sector_alerts.alert: Time magazine's May 25 'Deepening Democratic Crisis' explainer and pro-Kurdish DEM Party condemnation confirm the Turkey arc has entered a sustained, internationally-characterized democratic crisis. Protests ongoing across Turkey. CHP leader Özel led march toward Parliament after emerging from the seized headquarters. The three-event arc — Imamoglu imprisonment (March 2025), judicial CHP leadership removal (May 21), physical HQ seizure (May 24) — is now internationally recognized as a systematic suppression pattern. Assessor must determine whether any of the post-May 23 arc events constitute a second scored event on top of the -4.9 apply. Turkey working baseline: 17.6 (Critical).

sector_alerts.sector: EU Rule of Law — Slovakia Conditionality Mechanism Escalation
sector_alerts.alert: European Parliament voted 418-207 on May 20, 2026, to formally urge the European Commission to trigger the EU Rule of Law Conditionality Mechanism against Slovakia — potentially freezing €15B in EU funds. This parallels the mechanism previously used against Hungary. EU Parliament cited: special prosecution office dissolution, corruption penalty cuts, Whistleblower Protection Office abolition attempt, media/civil society restrictions, Agricultural Agency fund misuse. The Renew Europe Group separately demanded investigation into 'guesthouse projects' (EU funds for luxury private estates). Slovakia was last assessed May 22 at 39.1 Developing. This EP vote post-dates that assessment and must be evaluated for a second apply.

sector_alerts.sector: Gaza/Palestine — Aid Curbs During Ceasefire, Ongoing Civilian Deaths
sector_alerts.alert: HRW May 19 report documents: 856 Palestinians killed and 2,463 wounded since October 2025 ceasefire; only 50% of aid trucks allowed to unload in first 11 days of May; February 28 closure of all crossings (during Israel-Iran operations) reduced weekly truck average from 4,200 to 590. OCHA reports 10% funding gap for 2026 Flash Appeal ($4B needed). OHCHR Türk: Palestinians unsafe 6 months after ceasefire. ICJ: Israel's aid ban is inconsistent with international law. ICC arrest warrants for Netanyahu and Gallant. Total deaths: 72,619+ Palestinians from October 2023 to May 6, 2026. Palestine remains a founder-decision editorial hold but new institutional evidence accumulates.

sector_alerts.sector: AI Labs — CAISI Multi-Lab Evaluation (Microsoft, Google DeepMind, xAI)
sector_alerts.alert: The Commerce AI Center for Standards and Innovation (CAISI/NIST) will conduct classified environment security evaluations of AI models from Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI. This is a new US government regulatory engagement across multiple AI labs — all three are benchmarked entities. For Google DeepMind and Microsoft: CAISI cooperation is a transparency-positive signal. For xAI: being included in the same evaluation cohort suggests US government views xAI's security as requiring the same level of scrutiny as major labs, despite the CSAM arc. Assessor should note CAISI evaluation as a governance-engagement signal for all three affected entities.

sector_alerts.sector: Kenya — AI Labor Exploitation Arc (Meta/Sama + Global Alliance)
sector_alerts.alert: Meta terminated its Sama (Kenya) content moderation contract; worker rights group alleges retaliation. Global Trade Union Alliance of Content Moderators launched in Nairobi (workers from 9 countries). Kenya AI Bill 2026 introduced but excludes worker voice from governance structures. Jacobin May 2026 and IHRB call content moderation 'a new factory floor of exploitation.' Kenya at 41.8 Functional with active EMP-dimension evidence for first-baseline assessment.

forward_signals.label: Hungary — Magyar in Brussels to sign EU funds agreement. Agreement window closes if not signed by end of week. If signed: upgrade thesis confirmed with EC enforcement mechanism. Monitor for confirmation.

Hungary — Sulyok May 31 resignation deadline. Sulyok has refused; Magyar threatens constitutional amendment via supermajority. Constitutional confrontation outcome is a governance-dimension signal either way.
Vanuatu — Pacific cluster second resolution monitoring deadline. Primary UNGA resolution applied May 23. Secondary Pacific SIDS actions monitoring window closes.

DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic-Pentagon case. Oral arguments May 19 with strong panel skepticism. Cycle 6 hold on band-crossing-proposed (Functional→Established at 60.0). Ruling expected within weeks.

Turkey — Protest suppression watch. If Turkish security forces arrest protesters or use violence against CHP march toward Parliament, additional HAR-dimension events would accumulate. Monitor 48-72 hour window.

Slovakia — European Commission response to May 20 EP conditionality resolution. If EC opens formal conditionality investigation: that would be a Tier-1 action warranting a second formal apply for Slovakia.
Colombia — OHCHR Expert Mechanism full report to UN HRC 63rd session. Sub-threshold accumulator -3.5 from 44.5 may cross apply threshold before then.
United States — OBBBA Medicaid work requirement re-verification begins December 2026. CBO: 11.8M Americans lose health insurance. Active implementation accumulation arc.

methodology_novel_flags.description: Turkey's May 24 police action (riot police storming CHP headquarters, tear gas, rubber bullets) was documented in May 24 scan as potentially a second scored event from the May 23 apply. May 25 scan confirms: international media now characterizes the arc as a 'deepening democratic crisis' (Time magazine). Protests have mobilized across Turkey. Pro-Kurdish DEM Party has formally condemned. The mass public response is a new-dimension signal — not captured in either the May 21 court ruling or the May 24 police action alone. The protest mobilization scale is a measure of democratic society's response to the suppression arc.
methodology_novel_flags.assessment_implication: Assessor should determine: (1) Does mass protest mobilization constitute an additional scored signal, or is it societal response captured within the governance events already scored? (2) If protest suppression occurs (arrests, violence against protesters), that would be a distinct new scored event. (3) The international 'deepening democratic crisis' characterization may strengthen the case for treating the arc as a second scored event from 17.6. Turkey floor-of-scale question: assessor should document whether any practical minimum applies.

methodology_novel_flags.description: The European Parliament voted 418-207 on May 20, 2026, to urge the EU conditionality mechanism against Slovakia — the same mechanism used against Hungary under Orbán. This creates a precedent question: if the conditionality mechanism is triggered against Slovakia, how does this interact with the simultaneously-positive Hungary arc (Magyar seeking to satisfy conditionality requirements to unlock €10.4B)? The two countries are moving in opposite directions under the same regulatory instrument. For benchmarking: the EP resolution is a formal EU institutional action that post-dates Slovakia's May 22 assessment. Assessor must determine: is a 418-207 EP resolution urging conditionality a Tier-1 apply-threshold event or a Tier-2 accumulation event pending Commission decision?
methodology_novel_flags.assessment_implication: Recommended interpretation: the EP 418-207 resolution is a Tier-1.5 event — stronger than a single MEP statement or political party demand, but weaker than a Commission formal investigation. It should be treated as a confirmed accumulation event that, combined with the documented Dismantler evidence (May 22 apply of -5.5), provides basis for a second sub-threshold accumulation toward a potential third apply. Slovakia at 39.1 Developing. Any future Commission formal investigation would be apply-threshold.
